mnemonics: Servlet Lifecycle: S.I.D.D. (Service, Init, Destroy, doGet/doPost) - Like a 'SIDD'ing into web requests
keyPoints: A servlet is a Java class that extends HttpServlet and handles HTTP requests.
The servlet lifecycle is managed by the container through init(), service(), and destroy() methods.
The doGet() method handles HTTP GET requests, while doPost() handles HTTP POST requests.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call super.doGet() or super.doPost() when overriding methods, which can break default functionality.
Not properly handling both GET and POST requests, leading to '405 Method Not Allowed' errors.
Mixing business logic with presentation logic (HTML output) inside the servlet, instead of using JSPs or templating.
